title: 10 best herbal capsules for constipation

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Psyllium capsules helps with constipation because they contain a soluble fiber that absorbs water in the digestive system, softening stool and making it easier to pass. This helps to promote regular bowel movements and relieve symptoms of constipation such as bloating, discomfort, and straining. Additionally, psyllium also increases the bulk of stool, which can help to stimulate the muscles in the intestines to contract and move stool through the digestive system more efficiently.
remedy_properties: Fiber content: Psyllium husk, the main ingredient in herbal psyllium capsules, contains a high amount of soluble fiber that helps to soften stool and increase its bulk, making it easier to pass through the digestive system.
Prebiotic properties: Psyllium feeds good bacteria in the gut, promoting their growth and activity, which helps to regulate bowel movements and improve digestion, reducing symptoms of constipation.

Viscosity-modifying effects: The soluble fiber in psyllium capsules absorbs water and expands in the digestive system, increasing stool viscosity and helping to slow down its passage through the colon, allowing for a more complete evacuation of the bowels.
remedy_constituents: Psyllidin: This alkaloid helps to increase bowel movements and soften stool, making it easier to pass, and also has a mild laxative effect.
Tannins: The tannins present in Plantago ovata help to increase the water content in the stool and relax the muscles in the intestines, making it easier to have a bowel movement.
Fiber (Mucilage): The mucilage in Plantago ovata forms a protective barrier on the intestinal mucosa, helping to retain water, soften stool, and ease the passage of stool.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used to make capsules for constipation due to their high mucilage content, which helps to soften stool and promote regular bowel movements.
Leaves: Used to make capsules for constipation as they are rich in mucilage, which helps to protect and soothe the gastrointestinal tract.
Roots: Used to make capsules for constipation due to their high concentration of mucilage and fiber, which helps to regulate bowel movements and prevent constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago ovata leaves and stems to use for herbal capsule making.
Dry the harvested plantago ovata leaves and stems in a low-temperature dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind 2 cups of dried plantago ovata into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the ground plantago ovata powder with 2 tablespoons of gelatin to create a mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the plantago ovata gelatin mixture and seal the capsules using a capsule sealer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian capsules helps with constipation because they contain a natural bitter compound that stimulates digestive enzymes and increases bowel movement. The capsule's active ingredients, such as yellow gentian root extract, work to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing stool to pass more freely and regularly. Additionally, the herbal remedy also helps to reduce inflammation and spasms in the colon, which can contribute to constipation. As a result, yellow gentian capsules provide a natural and effective way to alleviate symptoms of constipation and promote regular bowel movements.
remedy_properties: Stimulant action: Yellow gentian's stimulant property helps to increase peristalsis, or muscle contractions, in the digestive tract, which can help move stool through the intestines more efficiently and alleviate constipation.

Anti-inflammatory effect: Gentian's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the gut, which can contribute to constipation by reducing muscle contractions and increasing mucous production, making stool harder to pass.

Bitter taste: The bitter compounds present in yellow gentian capsules can stimulate the appetite, improve digestion, and increase the movement of food through the digestive system, all of which can help alleviate constipation.
remedy_constituents: Gentianine: This alkaloid helps with constipation by stimulating the digestive system and increasing gut motility, promoting regular bowel movements.
Gentianic acid: This phenolic compound has laxative properties, helping to soften stool and stimulate bowel movements, relieving constipation.
Xanthones: These compounds, specifically gentisin and gentisic acid, have been found to have laxative effects, helping to regulate bowel movements and alleviate constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Gentiana lutea roots are used to make capsules due to their high content of iridoids, particularly gentiopicroside, which has laxative properties.
Rhyzomes: Gentiana lutea rhyzomes are used to make capsules due to their ability to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.
Seeds: Gentiana lutea seeds are used to make capsules due to their rich content of gentiopicrin, which has a laxative effect.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of dried gentiana lutea roots and stems from a reputable source.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of the powder with 50 grams of gelatin in a bowl for 2 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler to dissolve the gelatin at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 20 minutes.
Fill 100 empty capsules with the cooled mixture and seal them tightly using a capsule filler for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with constipation because they stimulate the digestive system to move food through the intestines more efficiently. The capsules contain bioactive compounds that increase gut motility, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Additionally, dandelion's diuretic properties help eliminate excess water in the body, reducing bloating and discomfort associated with constipation. By promoting regular bowel movements, dandelion capsules can provide relief from chronic constipation and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Choleretic property: Dandelion capsules stimulate the liver to produce more bile, which helps to soften and move stool through the digestive system, relieving constipation.
Laxative property: The inulin content in dandelion capsules acts as a natural prebiotic, feeding beneficial gut bacteria and promoting regular bowel movements and reducing constipation symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory property: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds may help soothe and calm irritated digestive tissues, reducing inflammation that can contribute to constipation and improving overall bowel health.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpenoid saponin helps with constipation by promoting the relaxation of the intestinal smooth muscle, which in turn enhances intestinal motility and improves bowel movements.

Alkaloids (Taraxacum alkaloids): These compounds, including taraxasterol and caffeic acid, exhibit a laxative effect by stimulating the contraction of intestinal muscles and increasing the secretion of water and electrolytes in the intestines, thereby softening stool and promoting bowel movements.

Flavonoids (including inulin): Inulin, a fructan, acts as a prebiotic, promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which can improve digestion, enhance the absorption of nutrients, and stimulate bowel movements, ultimately helping to alleviate constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain inulin, a type of soluble fiber that helps promote bowel movements and alleviate constipation.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and other bioactive compounds that may help stimulate the digestive system and relieve constipation.
Seeds: They contain inulin and other prebiotic fibers that can help feed beneficial bacteria in the gut, promoting a healthy gut microbiome and regular bowel movements.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 fresh taraxacum officinale roots with a fork and dry them in a warm place for 2 weeks.
Grind 2 teaspoons of dried taraxacum officinale roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 2 teaspoons of the taraxacum officinale powder with 1 tablespoon of gelatin and 2 tablespoons of water in a bowl.
Pour the mixture into empty gelatin capsules and seal them with a capsule sealer for 10 seconds.
Label the capsules with the contents and date and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salad burnet capsules helps with constipation because of its unique ability to stimulate digestive processes and soften stool. The herb's natural properties, particularly its saponin content, help to increase bowel motility and alleviate symptoms such as bloating and discomfort. Additionally, salad burnet's anti-inflammatory effects may reduce irritation and inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, allowing for a smoother passage of waste through the system and promoting regular bowel movements. This holistic approach can provide long-lasting relief from constipation symptoms.
remedy_properties: Sennoside: Herbal salad burnet capsules contain sennosides, which are natural laxatives that help stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation by increasing the frequency of bowel movements.

Tannins: The tannins present in herbal salad burnet capsules have astringent properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, ease bowel irritation, and promote regular bowel movements.
Anthraquinones: Anthraquinones in herbal salad burnet capsules help increase the water content of stool, making it softer and easier to pass, which can help alleviate constipation symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Help with constipation by increasing the water-holding capacity of stool, making it softer and easier to pass.
Flavonoids: Contribute to the relief of constipation by improving gut motility and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract.
Astragalin: A type of flavonoid glycoside, which may help alleviate constipation by increasing the secretion of digestive enzymes and improving the overall digestive process.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Sanguisorba minor leaves are used to make capsules for constipation because they contain anthraquinones, which have a laxative effect.
Roots: Sanguisorba minor roots are used to make capsules for constipation because they contain tannins and anthraquinones that help stimulate bowel movements.
Stems: Sanguisorba minor stems are used to make capsules for constipation because they contain anthraquinones, similar to the leaves and roots, which help relieve constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried sanguisorba minor root and leaf material from a reputable supplier or your own garden.
Dry the material at 50-60 degrees celsius for 2 hours to ensure a consistent moisture level.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powdered sanguisorba minor with 2 parts of gelatin capsules to create the final product.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule filling machine or a manual filling tool.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with constipation because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has natural laxative properties. The herb stimulates the digestive system to move food through the colon and increases bowel frequency. Additionally, licorice root has anti-inflammatory effects that may help soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of constipation such as bloating and discomfort. Regular use of herbal licorice capsules can promote regular bowel movements and alleviate constipation-related issues.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and promoting regular bowel movements.

Flavonoids: Licorice root contains flavonoids, such as liquiritin and isoliquiritigenin, that have been shown to improve gut motility and enhance the absorption of water in the intestines, helping to prevent constipation.

Inulin: Herbal licorice capsules often contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that feeds good bacteria in the gut, promoting a healthy gut microbiome and supporting regular bowel movements by increasing stool bulk and softening stools.
remedy_constituents: Licoricidin: Acts as a laxative by stimulating bowel movements and increasing the frequency of defecation, helping to alleviate constipation.
Glycyrrhizin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which may help soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the intestines, allowing for smoother bowel movements.
Flavonoids (specifically, liquiritigenin): Have a relaxing effect on the smooth muscle of the intestines, promoting regular bowel movements and reducing the risk of constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound responsible for the laxative effect, which helps alleviate constipation.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, making them a popular choice for constipation relief.
Seeds: Although less commonly used, the seeds of Glycyrrhiza glabra are sometimes used due to their lower glycyrrhizin content, which can still provide a gentle laxative effect.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 to 30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reliable source.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powder with 2 parts of gelatin and 1 part of water to form a uniform paste.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 500mg of the glycyrrhiza glabra paste and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Puncture vine capsules helps with constipation because they contain the herb Tribulus terrestris, which has natural laxative properties. The capsules stimulate bowel movements and promote regularity by increasing gut motility and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract. This allows waste to move more efficiently through the intestines, reducing symptoms of constipation such as bloating, discomfort, and difficulty passing stools. By supporting healthy digestion, puncture vine capsules can help restore a balanced digestive system and alleviate constipation.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Saponins, a type of triterpene glycoside, have been shown to increase gut motility and relax smooth muscle in the digestive tract, helping to stimulate bowel movements and alleviate constipation.

Flavonoids and phenolic acids: The flavonoid and phenolic acid compounds present in Puncture Vine Capsules have antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is often associated with constipation.
Anthraquinones: Anthraquinones, a type of glycoside, are known to stimulate the muscles in the intestinal wall, helping to increase bowel movements and relieve constipation.
remedy_constituents: Steroids: Steroids in Tribulus terrestris, particularly furostanol saponins, have been shown to increase gut motility and improve bowel movements, helping to alleviate constipation.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Tribulus terrestris, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract and promote regular bowel movements.

Saponins: Saponins in Tribulus terrestris, including tribuloside and protodioscin, have been found to stimulate the contraction of intestinal muscles, enhance gut motility, and increase the frequency of bowel movements, making them beneficial in relieving constipation.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are commonly used due to their high saponin content, which is believed to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.
Roots: The roots of Tribulus terrestris are also used for constipation relief, possibly due to their ability to increase water and electrolyte absorption in the intestines.
Seeds: Some sources also support using the seeds of Tribulus terrestris for constipation, possibly due to their stimulant and purgative properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500g of dried tribulus terrestris root and stem material.
Grind 200g of the dried material into a fine powder using a mill.
Mix the powder with 100g of gelatin and 50g of vegetable glycerin in a bowl.
Fill 100 empty capsules with the mixture using a powder filling machine.
Label and date 100 capsules with the contents and recommended dosage of 2 capsules per day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with constipation because they contain a potent mixture of bioactive compounds, including vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that stimulate bowel movements. The gel-like substance inside the aloe leaf is rich in mucilage, which soothes and lubricates the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can cause constipation. Additionally, the antioxidants present in aloe capsules help to soften stool and increase gut motility, allowing for regular and comfortable bowel movements.
remedy_properties: Aloin: A natural laxative, aloin helps stimulate bowel movements by increasing water absorption in the colon and relaxing the muscles to facilitate stool passage.

Anthraquinones: These compounds have been shown to increase gut motility, reduce inflammation, and stimulate the release of neurotransmitters that regulate bowel function, all of which can help alleviate constipation symptoms.

Polysaccharides: Aloe capsules contain a type of fiber-rich polysaccharide called acemannan, which helps soften stool, make it easier to pass, and support the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, promoting a healthy gut microbiome.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These glycosidic compounds stimulate bowel movement and relieve constipation by increasing water and electrolyte absorption in the colon, making stools softer and easier to pass.
Aloin: A type of anthraquinone, aloin helps to stimulate the intestinal muscles and increase the frequency of bowel movements, alleviating constipation and promoting a regular digestive routine.
Fibrin: A type of soluble dietary fiber, fibrin helps to soften and bulk up stool, making it easier to pass and reducing the risk of constipation and other digestive issues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Aloe vera contain a gel-like substance with laxative properties that help to relieve constipation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Aloe vera are rich in anthraquinones, which stimulate bowel movements and help to ease constipation.
Buds: The buds of Aloe vera also contain anthraquinones that help to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves with 5-7 inches in length to ensure maximum potency and effectiveness.
Clean and dry the leaves in a well-ventilated area for at least 30 minutes to prevent bacterial growth.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10-15 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the aloe vera powder and seal them using a capsule filler for 5 minutes.
Store the herbal aloe vera capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with constipation because they contain a natural oil that soothes the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and relaxing the colon. This allows stool to move more freely through the intestines, making it easier for waste to pass and relieving symptoms of constipation such as bloating, discomfort, and straining. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties may also help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, further alleviating discomfort and promoting regular bowel movements.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's main property is its ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing stool to pass more easily and reducing symptoms of constipation such as bloating and discomfort.

Essential Oils: Peppermint essential oils have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the intestines, which can contribute to constipation, and also stimulate the release of digestive enzymes to aid digestion.

Volatile Compounds: The volatile compounds present in peppermint capsules, such as menthone and limonene, have a stimulating effect on the gut and improve intestinal motility, helping to move stool through the digestive system and alleviate constipation.
remedy_constituents: Volatile Oils (particularly Linalool and Linalyl Acetate): These compounds help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms that can contribute to constipation and improving overall gut motility.

Rosmarinic Acid (a phenolic compound): As an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, rosmarinic acid helps soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation that can cause constipation and promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria.

Menthol (a terpene): Menthol stimulates digestive tract contractions, increasing the movement of food through the digestive system and helping to relieve constipation by promoting regular bowel movements.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain high amounts of menthol, which helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and promote bowel movements.
Stems: The stems are used because they contain menthone, a compound that helps to ease digestion and relieve constipation symptoms.
Roots: The roots are used because they contain a higher concentration of menthol and menthone than the leaves or stems, making them a more potent ingredient for constipation relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of fresh leaves of mentha x piperita when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 25-30°c for 7-10 hours or until crispy.
Grind 5 grams of dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of the powdered leaves with 0.5 grams of gelatin and 0.5 grams of water to form a gel.
Encapsulate the gel mixture into empty capsules using a capsule machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with constipation because of their natural ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of bloating, gas, and discomfort. The ginger content in these capsules has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including constipation, by increasing gut motility and reducing inflammation in the intestines. As a result, herbal ginger capsules can help alleviate hard stools, reduce straining during bowel movements, and restore regular bowel function, promoting a healthy and comfortable digestive system.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of constipation.
Spasmolytic properties: Ginger's ability to relax smooth muscle fibers helps relieve spasms in the intestines and rectum, promoting regular bowel movements and reducing symptoms of constipation.
Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidant compounds help protect the gut from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to constipation.
remedy_constituents: Shogaols: These gingerol derivatives have a laxative effect, helping to stimulate bowel movements and alleviate constipation.
Gingerols: These compounds have a mild laxative effect, increasing the movement of food through the digestive system and promoting regular bowel movements.

Terpenes (specifically, ginger terpene): Terpenes help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and promoting the movement of stool through the intestines, thereby easing constipation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part for making capsules due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have natural anti-inflammatory and digestive properties that help alleviate constipation.

Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used for making capsules as they contain a similar composition of gingerols and shogaols as the rhyzomes, making them effective in treating constipation.

Leaves: Although less commonly used, the leaves of Zingiber officinale can be used to make capsules for constipation relief, as they contain smaller amounts of gingerols and other bioactive compounds that can help stimulate digestion.
remedy_recipe: Grind 10 grams of dried zingiber officinale rhizomes into a fine powder using a mill for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with 20 grams of gelatin and 5 grams of water in a bowl for 2 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 40 degrees celsius for 10 minutes to dissolve the gelatin.
Fill empty capsules with the gelatin mixture using a capsule filler machine for 5 minutes.
Store the capsules in a cool dry place at room temperature for 2 weeks before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with constipation because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric stimulates the digestive system, increasing motility and reducing inflammation in the gut. This helps to alleviate symptoms of constipation such as bloating, discomfort, and irregular bowel movements. Additionally, turmeric's natural laxative effects can help soften stool and make it easier to pass, promoting regular and healthy bowel movements.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help reduce inflammation in the gut, making it easier to pass stool and alleviate constipation.
Fiber content: Turmeric contains dietary fiber that helps increase stool bulk, soften stool texture, and promote regular bowel movements, reducing symptoms of constipation.

Gingerols and Shogaols: These bioactive compounds in turmeric have a natural stimulatory effect on the digestive system, increasing gut motility and helping to move food through the digestive tract more efficiently, which can help alleviate constipation.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps with constipation by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and promoting the movement of food through the intestines, thereby alleviating symptoms of constipation.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): It helps with constipation by inhibiting the growth of intestinal bacteria that can contribute to constipation, and also by promoting the contraction of intestinal muscles, which aids in the passage of stool.
Turmerone: It helps with constipation by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and improving the gut motility, allowing for the smooth passage of stool and preventing constipation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes are used to make capsules for constipation because they contain bioactive compounds like curcumin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help regulate bowel movements.
Roots: Turmeric roots are used to make capsules for constipation because they contain fiber, which helps to soften stool and improve digestion.
Leaves: Turmeric leaves are used to make capsules for constipation because they contain fiber and antioxidants that help to regulate bowel movements and prevent constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kilogram of dried turmeric roots and store them in a cool dark place for future use.
Grind 200 grams of the dried turmeric roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the ground turmeric powder with 10 grams of gelatin and 5 milliliters of glycerin in a bowl.
Use a capsule machine to fill 60 empty capsules with the turmeric mixture in about 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years or use them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Barberry capsules helps with constipation because they stimulate the digestive system, increasing bowel movement frequency and regularity. The herb contains berberine, a compound that has been shown to improve gut motility and enhance the absorption of nutrients. This can help to soften stool, making it easier to pass, and alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramping, and discomfort associated with constipation. Additionally, barberry capsules may also help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, further contributing to its beneficial effects on bowel regularity.
remedy_properties: Anthraquinone glycosides: Barberry capsules contain anthraquinone glycosides, such as berberine, which stimulate bowel movements and increase stool frequency by enhancing gut motility and water absorption.
Aloe-like compounds: Barberry also contains aloe-like compounds that help to soften stools, making them easier to pass and reducing the risk of constipation caused by hard or dry stools.

Antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents present in barberry capsules may help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to constipation, and also protect against oxidative stress that can impair gut function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the barberry plant contain berberine, a natural compound with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that helps to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.

Rhyzomes: Barberry rhyzomes are rich in alkaloids and have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including constipation, due to their ability to stimulate the intestines and improve digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh berberis vulgaris roots and wash them thoroughly with cold water.
Dry the roots in a warm oven at 60°c for 2 hours or until crispy.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 250mg of the powdered root extract with 500mg of magnesium stearate and store in airtight capsules.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture, seal, and label clearly indicating dosages at 2-3 capsules thrice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal capsules helps with constipation because they contain berberine, a natural compound that has been shown to stimulate bowel movements and improve digestion. Berberine works by increasing the production of digestive enzymes, reducing inflammation in the gut, and promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria. As a result, goldenseal capsules can help to soften stool, increase its frequency, and alleviate symptoms such as bloating and discomfort associated with constipation.
remedy_properties: Berberine: A bioactive alkaloid that helps to reduce inflammation in the gut, which can contribute to constipation by slowing down digestion and reducing bowel movements.
Coptisine: A glycoside that has been shown to stimulate the muscles of the digestive tract, increasing peristalsis and promoting regular bowel movements.

Berberastatin: A compound that has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, leading to improved digestion and reduced constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Goldenseal plant are rich in berberine, a natural stimulant that helps to increase bowel movements and relieve constipation.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of Goldenseal contain high concentrations of berberine, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract and ease constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried goldenseal root hydrastis canadensis from a reputable source.
Grind the dried goldenseal root into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 10 grams of the powdered goldenseal root with 50 milliliters of organic virgin coconut oil and stir well.
Fill gelatin capsules with the goldenseal root mixture, using 250 milligrams per capsule and prepare 100 capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregon grape capsules helps with constipation because they contain anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate the muscles in the digestive tract to contract and move food through the intestines more quickly. Additionally, the capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with constipation. Regular use of Oregon grape capsules can promote regular bowel movements and alleviate symptoms of constipation, providing relief from bloating, abdominal pain, and hard stools.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help to stimulate bowel movements, reduce inflammation, and promote regularity by increasing the production of digestive enzymes and improving gut motility.

Berberine: This alkaloid compound has natural laxative effects, stimulating the gut muscles to contract and move stool through the intestines, while also reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy gut microbiome.

Quercetin: As an anti-inflammatory flavonoid, quercetin helps to soothe and protect the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to constipation, while also promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anthraquinones, which stimulate bowel movements and help relieve constipation.
Rhyzomes: Containing sennosides, these underground stems have been traditionally used to treat constipation by stimulating the bowels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of fresh mahonia aquifolium leaves and stems from a trusted source.
Dry the harvested material in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture levels.
Grind 250 grams of dried mahonia aquifolium into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground herb with 125 milliliters of glycerin and 125 milliliters of distilled water to create a liquid extract, allowing it to steep for 2 hours.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the liquid extract and seal them tightly, ready for consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm capsules helps with constipation because of its mucilaginous properties, which soothe and lubricate the digestive tract. The capsule's natural fibers help to soften stool and reduce inflammation in the colon, making it easier for waste to pass through the digestive system. Additionally, slippery elm's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, promoting regular bowel movements and reducing the frequency of constipation. As a result, slippery elm capsules can provide natural relief from constipation and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Slippery elm contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and lubricate the digestive tract, helping to soften stool and reduce inflammation.

Demulcent Action: The demulcent action of slippery elm helps to create a protective barrier on the surface of the intestines, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with constipation and promoting regular bowel movements.
Fiber Content: Slippery elm is a natural source of dietary fiber, which can help regulate bowel movements, prevent constipation by adding bulk to stool, and promote a healthy gut microbiome.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the slippery elm plant are used to make medicinal capsules because they contain mucilages that help soothe and protect the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by constipation.

Barks: The barks of the slippery Elm plant are used to make medicinal capsules as they contain a high concentration of mucilage, which helps to soften stool and reduce straining during bowel movements.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ulmus rubra bark, drying it thoroughly for at least two hours before use.
Grind dried ulmus rubra bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one teaspoon of the ground bark powder with enough water to create a paste, stirring well to combine.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the ulmus rubra bark paste, ensuring each capsule contains exactly 500 milligrams.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or freeze for longer preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with constipation because they contain fiber-rich extracts that promote digestive regularity and ease bowel movements. The seeds of the fennel plant have natural lubricating properties, which help to soften stool and prevent it from becoming too dry or hard to pass. Additionally, fennel's natural carminative effects help to reduce bloating and discomfort associated with constipation, making it a gentle yet effective natural remedy for maintaining regular bowel function.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Fennel's carminative property helps to reduce gas and bloating, which can alleviate discomfort and cramping associated with constipation, allowing for a smoother passage of stool.

Stimulant property: Fennel's stimulant property stimulates the muscles in the intestines, increasing bowel movements and helping to eliminate waste more effectively, reducing the risk of constipation.
Soothing property: Fennel's soothing property helps to calm inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms like pain, discomfort, and spasms associated with constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are used in herbal remedies to help stimulate bowel movements and relieve symptoms of constipation due to their bitter compounds.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a number of essential oils, including anethole, which can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce symptoms of constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare seeds or dried foeniculum vulgare powder from a trusted source weighing approximately 200 grams.
Cleanse the harvested material by sifting it through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any debris or impurities.
Dry the cleaned material in a warm oven at 60 degrees celsius for two hours or until completely dry and brittle.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle with approximately 100 grams of powdered cornstarch added.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with one-quarter teaspoon of the prepared powder and seal tightly for optimal shelf life.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for constipation are natural supplements that help alleviate symptoms of constipation by regulating bowel movements and improving digestive health. The best herbal capsules for constipation include Plantago ovata, Cassia angustifolia, Rheum palmatum, Magnolia officinalis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Aloe barbadensis, Foeniculum vulgare, Trifolium pratense, Terminalia chebula, and Aloe vera. If left untreated, constipation can lead to severe discomfort, fatigue, and decreased productivity, negatively impacting daily life, social relationships, and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to constipation that are treated with herbal capsules are hemorrhoids, diverticulitis, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Herbal supplements like psyllium, flaxseed, and slippery elm help soothe and soften stool, reducing discomfort and pain. Additionally, herbs such as turmeric, ginger, and peppermint may alleviate symptoms of IBS, including bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. These herbal capsules work to regulate bowel function and promote overall digestive health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with constipation is a blend of Psyllium Husk, Flaxseed, and Triphala. Psyllium Husk helps to soften and bulk up stool, while Flaxseed adds moisture and lubrication. Triphala, a traditional Ayurvedic remedy, stimulates bowel movements and improves digestion. This combination works synergistically to promote regular bowel movements, reduce symptoms of constipation, and support overall gut health. Consult with a healthcare professional before taking any herbal supplements.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago ovata capsules helps with constipation because they contain a natural laxative called psyllium. Psyllium is a soluble fiber that absorbs water in the digestive system, forming a gel-like substance that softens stool and stimulates bowel movements. As a result, psyllium helps to increase the frequency and ease of bowel movements, reducing the discomfort and pain associated with constipation. This natural and gentle approach can help to regulate bowel movements and promote overall digestive health. Regular use can lead to long-term relief from constipation.
remedy_constituents: Apolysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates help to increase the bulk and water-holding capacity of stool, promoting regular bowel movements and softening stool.
Saponins: Saponins have a mild laxative effect by stimulating the intestinal muscles to contract and move stool through the digestive system.

Muco-polysaccharides: These mucilaginous compounds help to soothe and protect the intestinal lining, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy gut environment, which can aid in regular bowel movements and preventing constipation.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Plantago ovata are used to make capsules for constipation due to their high mucilage content, which helps to regulate bowel movements and soften stool.
Leaves: The leaves are used in some herbal remedies, but the seeds are the primary component of the capsules.

Seeds (again, as the seeds are the primary and most used part): The seeds are used to make capsules for constipation due to their high mucilage content, which helps to regulate bowel movements and soften stool.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried plantago ovata seeds and sift them through a fine mesh to remove impurities.
Grind 50g of the sifted seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 2g of glycerin with 50g of the powder in a small bowl and stir until well combined.
Fill a capsule machine with the mixture and prepare 100 capsules using 0.5g of mixture per capsule.
Store the capsules in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia angustifolia capsules helps with constipation because they are rich in sennosides, which stimulate bowel movements and increase gut motility. The capsules help to soften and move stool through the digestive system, reducing the risk of constipation. Regular use of Cassia angustifolia capsules can also help to improve the regularity of bowel movements, making it easier to pass stool. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to promote digestive health and relieve constipation. It is a gentle yet effective solution.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: Stimulate intestinal motility and increase water secretion in the intestines, which helps to soften stool and facilitate its passage.
Flavonoids: Exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and promote regular bowel movements.
Saponins: Increase water absorption in the intestines and have a laxative effect by stimulating the muscles in the digestive tract to contract and move stool through the digestive system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make capsules for constipation due to their sennoside content, which acts as a laxative.
Seeds: Used to make capsules for constipation due to their high sennoside content, which stimulates bowel movements.
Roots: Used to make capsules for constipation due to their rich content of sennosides, which help in regulating bowel movements.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of cassia angustifolia roots from a well-established plant, ensuring no contamination or damage.
Dry the harvested roots in a well-ventilated area at 40-50°c for 24 hours to remove moisture.
Crush the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or grinder, taking care not to generate excessive heat.
Mix 10-15 grams of the powdered root with 10-15 grams of cellulose or starch as a filling material to form a uniform blend, ensuring accurate dosing.
Fill gelatin or vegetable capsules with 400-500 mg of the blended material using a capsule filler machine, ensuring accurate dosing and sealing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rheum palmatum capsules helps with constipation because of its ability to stimulate bowel movements and promote regular digestion. The herb, also known as Chinese rhubarb, contains anthraquinones that help to soften and move stool through the digestive system. This can help to alleviate symptoms of constipation, such as bloating, discomfort, and difficulty passing stool. Regular use of Rheum palmatum capsules may also help to improve overall digestive health and reduce the risk of constipation. This natural approach can provide relief and support for those experiencing constipation.
remedy_constituents: Rhein: Acts as a laxative by stimulating intestinal movement, increasing water and electrolyte absorption, and enhancing the elimination of waste products.
Anthraquinones (including Aloe-emodin and Emodin): Stimulate bowel movements by increasing intestinal motility and enhancing the secretion of water and electrolytes into the intestines.
Saponins: Help to relieve constipation by increasing the motility of the intestines, improving bowel habits, and reducing inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are primarily used due to their high concentration of anthraquinones, which stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are also used due to their anthraquinone content and are often harvested for their laxative properties to treat constipation.
Leaves: The leaves are used in traditional medicine for their laxative properties, although their effectiveness is generally lower than the roots and rhyzomes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rheum palmatum rhizome and roots in early morning when moisture content is highest weighing around 200 grams.
Dry the harvested rheum palmatum material in a well-ventilated area at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind dried rheum palmatum into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle taking around 30 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of rheum palmatum powder with 50 grams of gelatin in a bowl then add 100 milliliters of water stirring constantly.
Fill a capsule machine with the rheum palmatum gelatin mixture and seal the capsules weighing around 500 pieces.

remedies.remedy_desc: Magnolia officinalis capsules helps with constipation because it contains compounds that stimulate digestive function and promote bowel movement. The bark of the Magnolia tree has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat gastrointestinal issues, including constipation. The capsule's active ingredients, such as magnolol and honokiol, have been shown to increase the movement of food through the digestive system and enhance the contraction of intestinal muscles, making it easier to pass stools and relieve constipation symptoms. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to maintain regular bowel movements.
remedy_constituents: Magnolol: It helps by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and improving intestinal motility, thereby facilitating the movement of stool through the colon.
Honokiol: It helps by reducing inflammation and spasms in the digestive tract, which can contribute to constipation, and also has a soothing effect on the gut, promoting regular bowel movements.
Dihydromagnolol: It helps by inhibiting the contraction of smooth muscles in the intestinal tract, which can help to prevent constipation by allowing for a more regular flow of stool.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to make capsules for constipation due to their laxative properties.
Seeds: Used to make capsules for constipation due to their ability to stimulate bowel movement and relieve constipation.
Rhyzomes: Used to make capsules for constipation due to their laxative properties and ability to relieve constipation.
remedy_recipe: Dry the flowers of magnolia officinalis in a warm place for 7 to 10 days at a temperature of 30 to 40 degrees celsius.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powdered magnolia officinalis flowers with 1000 milligrams of a filler material like rice flour.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler for about 10 minutes.
Seal the filled capsules in a sterilized environment to prevent contamination and ensure potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with constipation because they contain saponins, which are natural laxatives that help stimulate bowel movements and soften stool. The anti-inflammatory properties in Glycyrrhiza glabra also soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to constipation. Additionally, the herb has been traditionally used to regulate the digestive system and promote healthy gut motility, making it a popular natural remedy for constipation relief. Regular use may help alleviate symptoms and promote regular bowel movements.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Acts as a natural laxative by increasing the water content in the intestines, softening stool, and stimulating bowel movements.
Flavonoids: Have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and promote regular bowel movements.
Saponins: Have a mild laxative effect, increase the movement of food through the digestive system, and help relieve constipation symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain glycyrrhizin, which helps to soften and move stool, making it easier to pass.
Leaves: The leaves of this plant are also a source of glycyrrhizin, which helps to stimulate digestion and relieve constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root from a reputable supplier.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the powder with 100g of gelatin in a stainless steel bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture using a capsule filler for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis capsules helps with constipation because they contain a concentrated form of aloe vera's gel, which has natural laxative properties. The gel contains mucilages, polysaccharides, and other compounds that stimulate bowel movements and help soften stool. As a result, Aloe barbadensis capsules can increase water absorption, reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, and promote regular bowel movements, alleviating constipation symptoms. This natural remedy can provide relief from constipation without the need for harsh medications.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds stimulate bowel movements, increase intestinal motility, and relax the muscles in the intestines, making it easier to pass stool.
Glucomannans: These soluble fibers help increase water absorption in the intestines, soften stool, and bulking up the stool to make it easier to pass, thus alleviating constipation.
Polysaccharides: These compounds help increase water absorption, promote the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, and stimulate bowel movements, which can help relieve constipation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are primarily used due to their high gel content, which is rich in mucilages that help soften stool and regulate bowel movements.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome, or underground stem, of Aloe barbadensis contains compounds that aid in digestion and relieve constipation.
Seeds: The seeds contain a compound called aloin, which has laxative properties that help stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 aloe barbadensis leaves of uniform size from mature plants to ensure potency.
Cut the leaves into small pieces to facilitate drying and later grinding.
Dry the aloe pieces in a single layer at 50-60 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind the dried aloe into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500mg of the aloe powder to achieve optimal dosage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare capsules helps with constipation because they contain the essential oils of the fennel plant, which have natural laxative properties. The active compounds in fennel, such as anethole and fenchone, stimulate digestion and relieve bloating, making it easier for waste to pass through the digestive system. Additionally, fennel capsules may help to relax the muscles in the intestinal tract, allowing for a more regular bowel movement and reducing the risk of constipation. This natural remedy promotes a healthy digestive system.
remedy_constituents: Fenchone: This monoterpene has a spasmolytic effect, which helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, making it easier to pass stools.
Anethole: This phenylpropene has a laxative effect, which helps to increase the movement of food through the digestive tract and stimulate bowel movements.

Ferulic acid: This phenolic acid has a gastroprotective effect, which helps to reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, making it easier to pass stools and alleviating constipation symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their carminative and antispasmodic properties that help relieve digestive issues, including constipation.
Leaves: The leaves are used because of their rich content of fiber and essential oils that aid in promoting bowel movements and relieving constipation.

Fruits: The fruits (fruits of the fennel plant are actually the seeds contained within the umbrella-like inflorescence) are used due to their high fiber content and ability to stimulate digestion and relieve constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems in the morning when the plant is at its highest essential oil content.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems in a single layer at 35-40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems into a fine powder using a grinder at 20-30 seconds per ounce.
Mix the ground foeniculum vulgare powder with a binding agent such as gelatin or starch in a 1:1 ratio by weight.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the foeniculum vulgare powder mixture using a capsule filler at 1 gram per capsule.

remedies.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense capsules helps with constipation because they contain the herb Red Clover, which is rich in isoflavones. Isoflavones have been shown to promote digestive health and relieve symptoms of constipation by stimulating bowel movements and improving gut motility. The prebiotic properties of Red Clover also help to support the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, further enhancing digestive regularity and reducing the risk of constipation. This natural remedy promotes a healthy gut and supports overall digestive well-being.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: These compounds help with constipation by enhancing gut motility, improving bowel habits, and promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria.

Phenolic acids: They contribute to constipation relief by increasing water and electrolyte absorption in the intestines, promoting regular bowel movements, and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.

Saponins: These compounds aid in constipation by stimulating the peristalsis (muscular contractions) in the intestines, increasing the movement of food through the digestive system, and promoting the elimination of waste.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Trifolium pratense are used to make capsules for constipation as they contain saponins and other compounds that help stimulate bowel movements.
Leaves: The leaves of Trifolium pratense are used to make capsules for constipation as they contain fiber and other compounds that help soften and move stool through the digestive system.
Roots: The roots of Trifolium pratense are used to make capsules for constipation as they contain saponins and other compounds that help stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried trifolium pratense flowers from a reputable source to ensure quality.
Crush the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the trifolium pratense powder with 2 parts of a binding agent like gum arabic for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the trifolium pratense and binding agent mixture.
Store the herbal capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years to maintain potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Terminalia chebula capsules helps with constipation because of its natural laxative properties. The herb is rich in fiber and tannins, which help to soften and regulate bowel movements. It also stimulates the digestive system and improves gut health by reducing inflammation and promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria. Additionally, Terminalia chebula capsules help to increase the production of digestive enzymes, ensuring that food is properly broken down and absorbed, leading to regular and effortless bowel movements. This results in a reduction in constipation symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help alleviate constipation by promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which aids in the digestion and movement of food through the intestines.
Phenolic Acids: They possess laxative properties, which help stimulate bowel movements and improve the overall motility of the digestive system.

Triterpenes: These compounds help regulate the gut microbiota balance, enhance the absorption of nutrients, and improve the overall digestive function, all of which contribute to preventing constipation.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Fruits are the primary source of Chebulic acid and Chebulinic acid, which act as natural laxatives and help to regulate bowel movements.
Barks: Barks of Terminalia chebula contain tannins, which have a gentle purgative effect and help to soften stool, making it easier to pass.
Seeds: Seeds of Terminalia chebula contain a rich source of fiber and antioxidants, which help to regulate bowel movements, prevent constipation, and maintain overall gut health.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of dried terminalia chebula powder with 1% moisture content to ensure accuracy.
Mix the powder with a 3:1 ratio of cellulose powder to fill the capsules.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder mixture and adjust the settings to 0.8 milliliters per capsule.
Seal the capsules using a capsule sealer and store them in a cool dry place.
Label the capsules with the recommended dosage of 2 capsules twice a day for 30 days.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera capsules helps with constipation because they contain the gel of the aloe plant, which has natural laxative properties. The gel contains compounds such as aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to soften and bulk up stool, making it easier to pass. Additionally, aloe vera capsules can help to stimulate bowel movements and improve gut health, reducing the risk of constipation. Regular consumption of aloe vera capsules may promote regular bowel movements and alleviate symptoms of constipation.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates help soften stool, making it easier to pass and relieving constipation.
Anthraquinones: These compounds stimulate bowel movements and help increase the frequency of defecation, thereby alleviating constipation.
Glycoproteins: These proteins help regulate bowel movements and improve gut health, which can contribute to the relief of constipation symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are commonly used to make capsules for constipation because they contain a gel-like substance rich in fiber and antioxidants.
Leaves: The leaves also contain aloin, a compound that helps stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.
Leaves: The gel from aloe vera leaves is also used to soothe and calm the digestive system, which can help alleviate constipation symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of aloe vera gel from mature leaves that have been properly cleaned and dried.
Dry the aloe vera gel at 50°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture and water content.
Weigh 10g of dried aloe vera powder and mix it with 1g of vegetable cellulose in a bowl.
Fill 100 empty capsules with the aloe vera and cellulose mixture using an encapsulation machine.
Label and store the aloe vera capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.
